commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed for the credential-import UX, public SDK surface, and contributor proof gate before any repair automation would be appropriate.; Needs attention: The diff handles sensitive credential import and has a concrete flag-precedence issue that can ignore an explicit auth opt-out.; Review finding: [P1] Honor --no-auth-credentials when secrets are included — src/commands/migrate.ts:72-73 (sha=ffd1e6e6df6069b902776341eec4bd63c0169004)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds interactive `openclaw migrate` auth credential prompting, Codex and Hermes auth-profile/config imports, migration conflict guards, docs updates, and a provider-auth SDK helper export.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: pass both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials`, and the new resolver keeps `includeSecrets: true` before checking the opt-out. I did not run the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for the credential-import UX, public SDK surface, and contributor proof gate before any repair automation would be appropriate.

target.title: Migrate auth credentials from Codex and Hermes
target.body:
## Summary

- Add interactive `openclaw migrate` credential prompting with yes selected by default, while keeping non-interactive `--yes` credential import explicit via `--include-secrets` and adding `--no-auth-credentials`.
- Migrate supported Hermes and Codex OAuth/API-key credentials into OpenClaw auth profiles and config, including OpenCode auth-store credentials for OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, and non-enterprise GitHub Copilot.
- Discover OpenCode auth beside the migrated Hermes home and via applicable `XDG_DATA_HOME`, while skipping unsupported surfaces such as web-search-only Perplexity model auth and OpenCode Copilot enterprise routing.
- Add conflict, TOCTOU, and credential-fingerprint guards so a changed source auth file cannot write the wrong OAuth account during apply.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/files-and-skills.test.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/commands/migrate/output.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- Synthetic CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Autoreview note: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` was attempted twice after the latest fixes; both Codex-engine runs wedged silently under `codex exec`. `--no-tools` is not supported by the Codex engine, so there is no clean autoreview result for the latest commit.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw migrate hermes --include-secrets` now imports supported auth credentials discovered from both the Hermes source and the adjacent OpenCode auth store, then writes usable OpenClaw auth profiles/config for those model providers.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch after `pnpm build`, using disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and synthetic Hermes/OpenCode auth stores with fake credential values only.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created `<tmp>/.hermes/auth.json` plus `<tmp>/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` containing synthetic OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, and GitHub Copilot credentials; ran `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`; then inspected the generated auth profile store with secrets redacted to booleans.
Evidence after fix: Real CLI output summarized from the disposable state, with secret values not printed:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 4,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 4,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 4
  },
  "migrated": [
    "auth:openai-codex",
    "secret:opencode:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:opencode-go:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:github-copilot:opencode-auth-json"
  ],
  "profiles": {
    "openai-codex:account-acct_synthetic": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "hasAccess": true,
      "hasRefresh": true,
      "accountId": "acct_synthetic"
    },
    "opencode:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "opencode-go:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode-go",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "github-copilot:github": {
      "type": "token",
      "provider": "github-copilot",
      "hasToken": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The migration wrote all four supported auth profiles from the synthetic OpenCode store and finished with zero errors or conflicts.
What was not tested: Live third-party OAuth refresh against real external accounts, OpenCode Copilot enterprise endpoint routing, and broad remote/Testbox lanes.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
